COUNCIL MEETING
A G E N D A
Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 5:00 P.m. - 8:00 P.m.
Council Chamber, City Hall
4949 Canada Way, Burnaby, BC
Code of Conduct - Glossary of Terms

CALL TO ORDER
 
2.
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
 
3.
ANNOUNCEMENTS
 

4.

Open Council Meeting Agenda for April 28, 2026
 
5.
ADOPTION OF MINUTES
 
5.1
Open Council Meeting Minutes April 14, 2026
[image] 
1.2026.04.14 Council Minutes DRAFT.pdf
6.
DELEGATIONS AND INVITED
PRESENTATIONS
 
None.
7.

7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL
AND REPLACEMENT
[image]

2026-04-28.pdf

Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

[image]

Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected
intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and
cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.
9.

CONSENT AGENDA

BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION -

Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

3.Attachment 2 - As-found report, Forest Lawn Funeral Home

EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025

[image] 
1.TC - Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection Safety

10.
INFORMATION REPORTS
 
None.
11.
BYLAWS
 
11.1
FIRST AND SECOND READING
 
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950
Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
[image] 
1.14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand
Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf

Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received
Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two
market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73
(Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail
and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of
the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and
add 362 additional market strata units from the development
concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional
seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In
addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to provide the
inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from
the previous rezoning approval. 
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
[image] 
1.14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535
Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use
multiple-family development with a double-height commercial
storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units,
ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the
applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations
for the site. 
(Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
[image] 
1.14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland
Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed
self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in
accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and
approved transitionary provisions.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)
11.2
FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD READING
 
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
[image] 
1.14811.Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961 Amendment
Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-
bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 

[image] 
1.14812. Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amend Bylaw
No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979
(e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick
scooter programs)
[image] 
1.14813 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 3, 2026 BBY Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 .pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
[image] 
1.14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and
improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026)

Subject to approval of item 7.3. 
12.
CORRESPONDENCE
 
No items of correspondence were received for Council's
decision. All other items of correspondence were published at
the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions
package dated April 28, 2026.
13.
OTHER BUSINESS 
 
13.1
SCHEDULING: PRCC Meeting
 
Recommendation:
THAT the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee meeting be
scheduled on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at 5:00 pm in the
Council Chamber.
14.
RELEASE OF CLOSED MEETING
DECISIONS
 

15.
ADJOURNMENT

briefs.title: Burnaby replaces Zoning Bylaw 1965 with updated Bylaw 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby has replaced its old zoning bylaw from 1965 with a new one, Bylaw 2026. Zoning bylaws control how land can be used. The new bylaw will change what types of buildings and activities are allowed in different areas of Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw 2026 to see how it affects your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation Seeks Year 2 Operating Funding
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) has requested funding for its second year of operations. This funding will support the BHA's ongoing work in providing and managing affordable housing within Burnaby.

briefs.title: 2026 Property Tax Rates Need Council Approval
briefs.summary: Municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) property tax rates for 2026 need approval from the local council. This is a standard yearly process.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation
briefs.locations: British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Newfoundland and Labrador

briefs.title: New Approving Officer to Be Appointed
briefs.summary: A new Approving Officer will be appointed. The Approving Officer is responsible for making decisions on land development and subdivision applications. This ensures that new developments meet local standards and regulations.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about development decisions in your area.

briefs.title: Burnaby: Retaining Wall Height Variance on 11th Avenue Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property on 11th Avenue. This permit allows the property owner to build retaining walls that are higher than what's normally allowed by city rules. A Development Variance Permit (DVP) lets a property owner get exceptions to certain zoning rules.

briefs.title: Insurance Contract Renewals Approved for 2026
briefs.summary: The government has approved awarding contracts for property and liability insurance programs for 2026. This ensures the continuation of these insurance programs.

briefs.title: Networking Software Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract for networking software has been awarded. This means the government has chosen a company to provide software that helps computers and other devices communicate with each other.

briefs.title: New Civic Facility Furniture Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract for new furniture for a civic facility has been awarded. This means a specific company has been chosen to supply the furniture.

briefs.title: External Audit Services Contract Change
briefs.summary: A change to the contract for external audit services is being considered. This means the terms of the agreement with the company that checks the organization's financial records could be updated.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is just an update on a potential contract change.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex: Geotechnical Consulting Services Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a change to a contract for geotechnical services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical services involve studying the soil and rock conditions of a site.

briefs.title: ECC Spring 2026 Community Grants Available
briefs.summary: The City of Edmonton is offering community grants for Spring 2026. These grants support projects that benefit the Edmonton community.
briefs.action_required: Review the grant criteria and submit your application before the deadline.

briefs.title: Burnaby Fees & Charges Bylaw: Mid-Year Fee Changes in 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby is proposing changes to its Fees and Charges Bylaw in mid-2026. This bylaw sets the fees for various city services and permits. The changes will affect the cost of some permits and services provided by the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Fees and Charges Bylaw when it is released in 2026.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service

briefs.title: PDC Approves Tenant Protection Framework and Bylaw
briefs.summary: The City of Port Coquitlam (PDC) approved a new Tenant Protection Framework and Bylaw. This aims to protect tenants from unfair evictions and renovictions (evicting tenants to do renovations). It also sets standards for landlords when redeveloping rental properties.
briefs.action_required: Review the new framework and bylaw to understand your obligations as a landlord.

briefs.title: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project Proposed
briefs.summary: The city council is considering improvements to the Southeast Bikeway. These changes could affect how people access businesses along the route during construction.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed changes and consider how they might impact your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to Change for Protected Intersections
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing its design standards for protected intersections in the Town Centre area. These changes will affect how new buildings and developments are designed near these intersections.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated design standards if you plan to build in Burnaby Town Centre.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Exempting Some Brentwood Properties from New Housing Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby is considering whether to exempt some properties in the Brentwood Park area from new rules that allow more small-scale, multi-unit housing (SSMUH). These new rules generally make it easier to build things like townhouses or small apartment buildings. A decision on the exemptions is pending.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby city council decisions regarding SSMUH exemptions in Brentwood Park.

briefs.title: Grand Promenade Rezoning Changes: Hotel/Restaurant Approved, More Units Added
briefs.summary: A zoning change (called a 'rezoning') was approved for the Grand Promenade area. The changes allow a hotel and restaurant instead of just offices. The rezoning also adds more housing units and changes how 'inclusionary zoning' (rules for affordable housing) works in the area.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about development in the Grand Promenade area.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves 12-Storey Mixed-Use Development at 6535 Kingsway
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a change to its zoning rules (called a "zoning bylaw amendment"). This change allows a developer to build a 12-storey building at 6535 Kingsway. The building will have both commercial space and 290 apartments.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Change: Self-Storage Building Permit at 2757 Norland Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended its zoning rules to allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. This means the city has approved a change to what can be built on that specific property.

briefs.title: E-Bike Pilot Program: Street and Traffic Bylaw Changes
briefs.summary: The city is changing its Street and Traffic Bylaw for a pilot program about e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This means new rules might be coming for how and where these vehicles can be used.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the pilot program's rules as they develop.

briefs.title: Parks E-Bike Pilot Program: Bylaw Amended
briefs.summary: A bylaw changed to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters in parks. The Parks Regulation Bylaw was amended to allow this.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw for rules about e-bikes and scooters in parks.

briefs.title: E-Bike, E-Scooter Pilot: Bylaw Enforcement Rules Updated
briefs.summary: The city is changing its bylaw enforcement rules for a pilot program involving e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This means the city can now enforce rules about where and how these devices are used.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated rules for e-bike and e-scooter use.

briefs.title: Burnaby Property Tax Rates Set for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby has set the property tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in the city. This bylaw decides how much property owners will pay in taxes for 2026. These taxes fund city services.
briefs.action_required: Review the new rates to plan for property tax payments.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation
